ELPAC Level 1
Lexile ≥1400L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Glittering or Dusty

STS-MS6-L1-1400P-MSELA6-BWC-MNA-02 • MS ELA Grade 6 • Impact of Word Choice on Meaning and Tone
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
This class is in a Build-a-World Challenge. They are building a story city inspired by real old cities of Morocco and Egypt, with a busy market, sand-colored towers, and glowing lanterns. Before they write about their city, they must pick the exact words that will set its tone: one team wants readers to feel wonder, and one word can change everything.
If the team swapped the word “glittering” for “dusty,” how would the feeling of their city change?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Layla doing?
Look at the picture. Name Layla's action and the object she places, in precise words.
2
What is Omar holding?
Look at the picture. Name the object Omar holds and its material, in precise words.
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Name the teacher's action and what she pours, in precise words.
4
What room is this? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Name this room with the most precise word you know.
5
Tell me about the word board.
Look at the picture. Name the board's contents with the most precise word you know.
6
What is Samira wearing?
Look at the picture. Name Samira's clothing item and its color, in precise words.
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: CCSS RL.6.4 — Analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
